schema.blocks_and_items: Objects keyed by the exact Java registry field name.
schema.entities: Objects keyed by the exact registered AdventureCraft entity class name.

source_policy.baseline: Current Java source only; no external-wiki prose is used.
source_policy.null_mode_notes: No mode-specific behavior was proven in the cited source.
source_policy.unknowns: An unknowns entry names behavior that the cited source does not establish; it must not be presented as fact.

lockedDoor.purpose: A key-locked wooden door assembled from contiguous blocks.
lockedDoor.behavior: Attacking it while the inventory contains the matching Gold Key consumes one key and marks the connected door blocks unlocked.
lockedDoor.mode_notes: Unlocked blocks become non-colliding and visually absent outside debug mode; debug mode keeps the door visible for editing.
interactions: Uses the doorKey item registered by AC_Items.

lockedBossDoor.purpose: A key-locked metal boss door assembled from contiguous blocks.
lockedBossDoor.behavior: Attacking it while the inventory contains the matching Silver Key consumes one key and marks the connected door blocks unlocked.
lockedBossDoor.mode_notes: Unlocked blocks become non-colliding and visually absent outside debug mode; debug mode keeps the door visible for editing.
interactions: Uses the bossKey item registered by AC_Items.

newMobSpawner.behavior: It can spawn its configured mobs on trigger activation and/or deactivation, and reset its spawned-mob state.
newMobSpawner.mode_notes: It is pickable and its configuration GUI is available only in debug mode; GUI use additionally requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

spawnBlock.behavior: A player entering it sets the world spawn position and spawn yaw; trigger activation also sets the spawn position.
spawnBlock.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode.
interactions: Responds both to player collision and trigger activation.

triggerBlock.purpose: An invisible player-presence trigger linked to a configured block region.
triggerBlock.behavior: Outside debug mode, player entry activates its configured area or resets that area; connected trigger blocks share activation state and release the area after the player leaves.
triggerBlock.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: The Cursor selection can become the target area.
It is a trigger source and deliberately cannot itself be triggered.

triggerDoor.purpose: A door whose presence is controlled by AdventureCraft triggers.
triggerDoor.behavior: Activation makes it rendered and collidable and plays the open sound; deactivation makes it hidden and non-colliding and plays the close sound.
triggerDoor.mode_notes: Debug mode keeps it rendered for editing.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

spikeBlock.behavior: Any entity inside its narrowed collision area is hurt for 10 damage.

triggerInverter.purpose: An invisible trigger source that outputs the inverse of its input state.
triggerInverter.behavior: Input activation removes its configured output area; input deactivation adds that area.
triggerInverter.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: The Cursor selection can become the output area.

triggerMemory.behavior: It latches its configured output area active on either input activation or deactivation, according to configuration, until reset.
triggerMemory.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: The Cursor selection can become the output area.
Its reset-on-death setting controls whether death resets the latch.

clipBlock.behavior: It uses AIR material while retaining the default tile collision shape and cannot be activated by AdventureCraft triggers.

redstoneTrigger.purpose: A bridge from redstone power to an AdventureCraft trigger area.
redstoneTrigger.behavior: Neighbor redstone power activates its configured area, or resets that area when configured; loss of power removes its trigger area.
redstoneTrigger.mode_notes: Its configuration GUI is limited to debug mode with an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: The Cursor selection can become the output area.
Reads neighboring redstone signals.

redstonePower.purpose: A bridge from an AdventureCraft trigger state to redstone power.
redstonePower.behavior: It emits direct and indirect redstone signal, light level 14, and red-dust particles while its position is trigger-active.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas and updates neighboring blocks on state changes.

bombableCobblestone.purpose: A color-variant cracked cobblestone block that AdventureCraft bombs can remove.
bombableCobblestone.behavior: Bomb explosions remove instances within the bomb destruction radius because the block is an AC_BlockBombable.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
Destroyed by AC_EntityBomb explosions.

bombableStone.purpose: A color-variant cracked stone block that AdventureCraft bombs can remove.
bombableStone.behavior: Bomb explosions remove instances within the bomb destruction radius because the block is an AC_BlockBombable.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
Destroyed by AC_EntityBomb explosions.

weather.purpose: An invisible trigger controller for world environment settings.
weather.behavior: On activation it can change precipitation, temperature offset, time of day, and time rate, with each change independently configurable.
weather.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

musicTriggered.purpose: An invisible trigger controller for streaming music.
musicTriggered.behavior: Activation plays the configured music with fade-out and fade-in values, or stops music when no track is configured.
musicTriggered.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

pushableBlock.behavior: It falls through air, fire, water, or lava and preserves its metadata while falling.
interactions: The Power Glove can push or pull it.
A same-color adjacent instance can activate Trigger Pushable.

timer.purpose: An invisible configurable delayed and timed trigger source.
timer.behavior: Input activation starts a configurable delay, then activates or resets the selected area for an active interval and enforces an inactive interval before it can reactivate.
timer.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Outputs an AdventureCraft trigger area selected with the Cursor.

message.purpose: An invisible trigger controller for a HUD message and sound.
message.behavior: Activation displays the configured non-empty message and plays the configured non-empty sound.
message.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

fan.purpose: The active state of a directional fan.
fan.behavior: Each tick outside debug mode it pushes entities and particles through up to four unobstructed blocks; redstone power swaps it to fanOff.
fan.mode_notes: Debug-mode use rotates its facing; debug mode suppresses its pushing behavior.
interactions: An equipped Umbrella doubles the fan's push coefficient for players.
Neighbor redstone power turns it off.

camera.behavior: Activation loads and starts its camera path, enables the cutscene camera, and applies the configured pause-game setting.
camera.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor and selects the active editing camera.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

lightBulb.purpose: An invisible configurable light source controlled by triggers.
lightBulb.behavior: Its metadata stores a light level set to 15 on placement; while trigger-active it emits no light, and otherwise emits the stored level.
lightBulb.mode_notes: It is pickable and its light-level GUI opens only in debug mode.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

fanOff.purpose: The inactive state of a directional fan.
fanOff.behavior: It does not push entities; removal of neighboring redstone power swaps it to the active fan while preserving facing metadata.
interactions: Neighbor redstone power selects between fan and fanOff.

script.purpose: An invisible trigger controller for JavaScript files.
script.behavior: Activation runs the configured on-trigger script in the block's persistent scope; deactivation runs its on-detrigger script and updates activation state.
script.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

store.behavior: Outside debug mode, use consumes the configured sell item amount, grants the configured buy item amount, decrements supply, and can pulse a trade trigger area.
store.mode_notes: Debug-mode use opens the store configuration GUI instead of trading.
interactions: Can emit a one-shot AdventureCraft trigger after a successful trade.

effect.purpose: An invisible trigger controller for particles and visual environment effects.
effect.behavior: While active it can emit configurable particles; activation can also change fog color/density, set an overlay, and replace or revert textures.
effect.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

darkness.behavior: It has no collision box, is not solid-rendered, blocks light by 2, and deliberately cannot be activated by triggers.
darkness.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode.

triggerPushable.purpose: A color-matched detector for adjacent Pushable Blocks.
triggerPushable.behavior: A same-metadata Pushable Block on any adjacent face activates its configured area or resets that area; removing the neighbor deactivates the output.
triggerPushable.mode_notes: Its configuration GUI is limited to debug mode with an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Works with pushableBlock and AC_IBlockColor metadata.
Outputs an AdventureCraft trigger area.

storage.purpose: An invisible snapshot-and-restore controller for a selected block region.
storage.behavior: Configuration captures blocks, metadata, and block entities from its selected region; trigger activation restores the saved region and updates it.
storage.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Uses a Cursor-selected region.
Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

healDamage.purpose: An invisible trigger controller that changes every player's health.
healDamage.behavior: Activation heals all players when the configured value is positive and directly hurts all players by its absolute value otherwise.
healDamage.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

teleport.purpose: An invisible trigger controller that teleports all players.
teleport.behavior: Activation moves every player to the configured X/Z and the first air block at or above the configured Y; it does nothing until a target is set.
teleport.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode; debug use with an empty hand or Cursor stores Cursor position one as the destination.
interactions: Uses the Cursor selection endpoint as its destination.
Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

url.purpose: An invisible trigger controller for a URL confirmation prompt.
url.behavior: Activation opens a URL request GUI when a non-empty URL is configured.
url.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Responds to AdventureCraft trigger areas.

npcPath.purpose: An invisible trigger controller that sends a selected NPC to the block.
npcPath.behavior: Activation paths the bound NPC to this block; on arrival it can pulse a configured trigger area.
npcPath.mode_notes: It is pickable and configurable only in debug mode; configuration use requires an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Binds an AC_EntityNPC and can output an AdventureCraft trigger pulse.

pillarStone.purpose: A decorative stone pillar with 16 metadata variants.
pillarStone.behavior: Top, bottom, and side faces use coordinated textures selected by metadata.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

pillarMetal.purpose: A decorative metal-sounding pillar with 16 metadata variants.
pillarMetal.behavior: Top, bottom, and side faces use coordinated textures selected by metadata.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

plant1.purpose: A decorative non-colliding plant family with 16 metadata variants.
plant1.behavior: It renders as crossed reeds with texture selected by metadata.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

trees.purpose: A decorative tree-model family with 16 texture variants and per-block tree settings.
trees.behavior: It is non-colliding, uses a custom tree render shape, and stores its tree configuration in a block entity.
trees.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode; its configuration GUI requires debug mode and an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

glassBlocks.purpose: A decorative transparent solid-block family with 16 metadata variants.
glassBlocks.behavior: It suppresses faces between blocks of the same ID and selects texture by metadata.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

cageBlocks.purpose: A decorative transparent cage-block family with 10 metadata variants.
cageBlocks.behavior: It suppresses faces between blocks of the same ID and selects texture by metadata.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

stoneBlocks1.purpose: The first decorative solid stone-texture bank, with 16 metadata variants.
stoneBlocks1.behavior: Metadata selects one of its registered texture variants.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

stoneBlocks2.purpose: The second decorative solid stone-texture bank, with 16 metadata variants.
stoneBlocks2.behavior: Metadata selects one of its registered texture variants.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

stoneBlocks3.purpose: The third decorative solid stone-texture bank, with 16 metadata variants.
stoneBlocks3.behavior: Metadata selects one of its registered texture variants.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

woodBlocks.purpose: A decorative solid wood-texture bank with 16 metadata variants.
woodBlocks.behavior: Metadata selects one of its registered texture variants.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
Hookshots can attach to it.

halfSteps1.purpose: A stone half-step bank with 16 metadata states.
halfSteps1.behavior: Even metadata places a lower slab and odd metadata an upper slab; paired metadata values select textures.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

halfSteps2.purpose: A metal-sounding half-step bank with 16 metadata states.
halfSteps2.behavior: Even metadata places a lower slab and odd metadata an upper slab; paired metadata values select textures.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

halfSteps3.purpose: A wood-sounding half-step bank with 16 metadata states.
halfSteps3.behavior: Even metadata places a lower slab and odd metadata an upper slab; paired metadata values select textures.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
Hookshots can attach to it.

tableBlocks.purpose: A decorative table family with 16 metadata variants.
tableBlocks.behavior: It uses a thin tabletop collision shape and a custom table renderer.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

chairBlocks1.purpose: The first decorative chair bank with texture and facing metadata.
chairBlocks1.behavior: It uses a custom chair renderer and rotates to the placer direction while preserving its variant group.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

chairBlocks2.purpose: The second decorative chair bank with texture and facing metadata.
chairBlocks2.behavior: It uses a custom chair renderer and rotates to the placer direction while preserving its variant group.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

chairBlocks3.purpose: The third decorative chair bank with texture and facing metadata.
chairBlocks3.behavior: It uses a custom chair renderer and rotates to the placer direction while preserving its variant group.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
unknowns: AC_Blocks registers 16 subtypes but does not install a dedicated AC_ItemSubtypes item for this third chair bank.

chairBlocks4.purpose: The fourth decorative chair bank with texture and facing metadata.
chairBlocks4.behavior: It uses a custom chair renderer and rotates to the placer direction while preserving its variant group.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
unknowns: AC_Blocks registers 16 subtypes but does not install a dedicated AC_ItemSubtypes item for this fourth chair bank.

ropes1.purpose: The first decorative rope bank, with five textures in three orientations.
ropes1.behavior: Metadata chooses a vertical, X-axis, or Z-axis rope shape and a texture group; the vertical state has no collision.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

ropes2.purpose: The second decorative rope bank, with five textures in three orientations.
ropes2.behavior: Metadata chooses a vertical, X-axis, or Z-axis rope shape and a texture group; the vertical state has no collision.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

chains.purpose: A decorative chain bank, with three textures in three orientations.
chains.behavior: It inherits rope orientation and collision behavior and chooses side-sensitive chain textures from metadata.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

ladders1.purpose: The first decorative climbable ladder bank with four texture variants.
ladders1.behavior: Metadata combines four variant groups with four wall-facing directions; it does not remove itself when neighbors change.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing in steps of four metadata values.

ladders2.purpose: The second decorative climbable ladder bank with four texture variants.
ladders2.behavior: Metadata combines four variant groups with four wall-facing directions; it does not remove itself when neighbors change.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing in steps of four metadata values.

ladders3.purpose: The third decorative climbable ladder bank with four texture variants.
ladders3.behavior: Metadata combines four variant groups with four wall-facing directions; it does not remove itself when neighbors change.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing in steps of four metadata values.

ladders4.purpose: The fourth decorative climbable ladder bank with four texture variants.
ladders4.behavior: Metadata combines four variant groups with four wall-facing directions; it does not remove itself when neighbors change.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing in steps of four metadata values.

lights1.purpose: A decorative non-colliding light family with 14 metadata variants.
lights1.behavior: It renders as crossed reeds and is registered with light emission 15.0F / 16.0F for every variant.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

plant2.purpose: The second decorative tree-model plant bank with 16 texture variants.
plant2.behavior: It is non-colliding, uses a custom tree render shape, and stores per-block tree configuration.
plant2.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode; its configuration GUI requires debug mode and an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

plant3.purpose: The third decorative tree-model plant bank with 16 texture variants.
plant3.behavior: It is non-colliding, uses a custom tree render shape, and stores per-block tree configuration.
plant3.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode; its configuration GUI requires debug mode and an empty hand or Cursor.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

overlay1.purpose: A thin decorative overlay bank with seven texture variants.
overlay1.behavior: It aligns a non-colliding overlay plane to the first adjacent solid face and selects texture by metadata.
overlay1.mode_notes: It is pickable only in debug mode.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.
Implements AC_ITriggerBlock, but the interface default reports it as not triggerable and the class defines no activation callback.

stairs1.purpose: The first wood-template stair bank with four texture variants.
stairs1.behavior: Placement stores facing in the low two metadata bits and the texture variant in the higher bits.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

stairs2.purpose: The second wood-template stair bank with four texture variants.
stairs2.behavior: Placement stores facing in the low two metadata bits and the texture variant in the higher bits.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

stairs3.purpose: The first cobblestone-template stair bank with four texture variants.
stairs3.behavior: Placement stores facing in the low two metadata bits and the texture variant in the higher bits.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

stairs4.purpose: The second cobblestone-template stair bank with four texture variants.
stairs4.behavior: Placement stores facing in the low two metadata bits and the texture variant in the higher bits.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

slopes1.purpose: The first wood-template slope bank with four texture variants.
slopes1.behavior: It shares stair placement and metadata behavior but uses the custom slope render shape.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

slopes2.purpose: The second wood-template slope bank with four texture variants.
slopes2.behavior: It shares stair placement and metadata behavior but uses the custom slope render shape.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

slopes3.purpose: The first cobblestone-template slope bank with four texture variants.
slopes3.behavior: It shares stair placement and metadata behavior but uses the custom slope render shape.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

slopes4.purpose: The second cobblestone-template slope bank with four texture variants.
slopes4.behavior: It shares stair placement and metadata behavior but uses the custom slope render shape.
interactions: Supports AC_IBlockColor editing.

boomerang.behavior: Use launches one only while the item is inactive; the projectile returns to its owner and restores the item's active metadata when removed.
interactions: The projectile carries encountered item entities, stuns encountered mobs for 20 ticks, and operates levers it passes through.

doorKey.purpose: The consumable key for Locked Doors.
doorKey.behavior: Attacking a Locked Door consumes one Gold Key and unlocks the connected door blocks.
interactions: Matched to lockedDoor by its item ID.

hookshot.purpose: A reusable hookshot usable from the main hand or offhand.
hookshot.behavior: Use launches a hook when the player is grounded, swimming, or supported by the other attached hook; using it again recalls that hook.
interactions: It attaches to logs and AdventureCraft wood/wood half-step blocks, pulls the player toward attached surfaces, and returns grabbed mobs or items.

heart.behavior: Adding it to player inventory consumes the stack and heals 4 health per item instead of storing it.
interactions: Can be produced by AdventureCraft mob-drop handling.

heartContainer.behavior: Adding it to player inventory consumes the stack, increases maximum health by 4 per item, and heals the player to the new maximum.

woodenShield.purpose: A held shield that enables player damage protection.
woodenShield.behavior: The player reports shield protection while it is in the selected hand or offhand and the offhand swing progress is zero.
interactions: Protection is implemented by the player mixin rather than the base Item class.
unknowns: The cited item-specific source does not define which damage paths honor protectedByShield.

bossKey.purpose: The consumable key for Boss Doors.
bossKey.behavior: Attacking a Boss Door consumes one Silver Key and unlocks the connected door blocks.
interactions: Matched to lockedBossDoor by its item ID.

bomb.behavior: Use consumes one item and launches a bomb entity with a 45-tick fuse; the blast damages and pushes nearby entities and removes AC_BlockBombable blocks within radius 3.
interactions: The bomb also detonates immediately when hurt.

bombArow.purpose: Consumable explosive ammunition for bows and dispensers.
bombArow.behavior: Using a bow with Bomb Arrows in either hand consumes one and launches AC_EntityArrowBomb; a dispenser also launches that entity for this item.
interactions: The registered Java field is intentionally recorded as bombArow, while its description ID is bombArrow.

powerGlove.purpose: A tool for moving Pushable Blocks horizontally.
powerGlove.behavior: Use on a Pushable Block launches it one block away from the clicked face; holding either Control key reverses the direction to pull it.
powerGlove.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: The destination must be air, liquid, or fire, and only one tracked falling block can be moved at once.

heartPiece.behavior: Adding it to inventory consumes it and accumulates pieces; each four pieces increase maximum health by 4 and heal the player to full.

umbrella.purpose: A held mobility and air-push tool.
umbrella.behavior: Holding it in either hand caps downward velocity at -0.2; grounded use pushes entities and particles in a forward hemisphere and starts a 10-tick cooldown.
interactions: Fans apply twice their normal push coefficient to a player holding an Umbrella.

lantern.purpose: A portable light source with consumable fuel.
lantern.behavior: While used as a light it advances damage toward its 1200-point capacity; when exhausted it consumes one Oil and resets, otherwise it stops lighting.
interactions: Player-held lighting is integrated by MixinPlayerEntity.

oil.behavior: One Oil is removed from inventory whenever an exhausted held Lantern refuels.
interactions: Consumed automatically by AC_ItemLantern or player-held lantern handling.

pistol.behavior: Use fires one damage-9 bullet with 0.05 spread and consumes one of 15 loaded shots; reload consumes Pistol Ammo to refill all missing shots and applies a reload delay.
interactions: Implements muzzle-flash lighting and the item reload/use-delay interfaces.

rifle.behavior: Use fires one damage-10 bullet with spread based on the remaining use timer, consumes one of 30 loaded shots, and adds upward recoil; reload consumes Assault Rifle Ammo to refill all missing shots.
interactions: Implements muzzle-flash lighting and the item reload/use-delay interfaces.

shotgun.behavior: Use fires fourteen damage-2 pellets with 0.12 spread and consumes one of seven loaded shells; each reload step consumes one Shotgun Ammo and restores one shell.
interactions: Implements muzzle-flash lighting and the item reload/use-delay interfaces.

pistolAmmo.behavior: Pistol reload removes one item for each missing loaded shot until the magazine is full or ammunition runs out.
interactions: Also used as the death loot ID of AC_EntityZombiePistol.

rifleAmmo.purpose: Reload ammunition for the Assault Rifle.
rifleAmmo.behavior: Rifle reload removes one item for each missing loaded shot until the magazine is full or ammunition runs out.
interactions: Also used as the death loot ID of AC_EntitySkeletonRifle.

shotgunAmmo.behavior: Each shotgun reload step removes one item and restores one missing shell.
interactions: Also used as the death loot ID of AC_EntitySkeletonShotgun.

harp.behavior: Use opens its music-sheet GUI; use on a sign plays the sign's song with the harp configuration.
interactions: Configured with the default +3 tuning used by AC_ItemInstrument.

guitar.behavior: Use opens its music-sheet GUI; use on a sign plays the sign's song with the guitar configuration.
interactions: Configured with the default +3 tuning used by AC_ItemInstrument.

snare.behavior: Use opens its music-sheet GUI; use on a sign plays the sign's song with the snare configuration.
interactions: Configured with the default +3 tuning used by AC_ItemInstrument.

pegagusBoots.purpose: Armor boots that grant extra movement abilities while equipped.
pegagusBoots.behavior: Equipping them in slot 36 enables wall jumping and one air jump; removing them disables both.
interactions: The registered Java field is pegagusBoots; documentation must preserve that field spelling while displaying the localized name Pegasus Boots.

cursor.purpose: A two-point region-selection tool used by AdventureCraft editors.
cursor.behavior: Left- or right-clicking blocks alternates selection endpoints, computes the inclusive minimum/maximum bounds, and reports the selected volume.
cursor.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item; several block configuration GUIs explicitly permit Cursor as the held item in debug mode.
interactions: Supplies bounds to area tools and configurable trigger blocks.

brush.purpose: A single-block metadata color/variant editing tool.
brush.behavior: Use increments the clicked AC_IBlockColor value; sneaking decrements it, and unsupported blocks produce a message.
brush.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Works only on blocks implementing AC_IBlockColor.

eraser.behavior: With both Cursor points set, use replaces every block in the inclusive selection with air.
eraser.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Requires a completed Cursor selection.
unknowns: This implementation does not explicitly record an undo action.

paintBucket.purpose: A region-wide metadata color/variant editing tool.
paintBucket.behavior: With both Cursor points set, use increments every AC_IBlockColor block in the selection; sneaking decrements them.
paintBucket.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Requires a completed Cursor selection and ignores blocks without AC_IBlockColor.

hammer.purpose: A region fill tool and fast block-breaking tool.
hammer.behavior: With both Cursor points set, use fills the selection with the clicked block ID and metadata; it also reports destroy speed 32 and can destroy special blocks.
hammer.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation; normal block breaking is separately restricted by the interaction manager outside debug mode.
interactions: Requires a completed Cursor selection.

wrench.behavior: With both Cursor points set, use replaces selected block IDs allowed by its switch with the clicked block ID and metadata, while preserving excluded IDs.
wrench.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Requires a completed Cursor selection.
unknowns: The source gives no semantic label for why the hard-coded excluded ID set is preserved.

npcStick.purpose: An NPC creation and configuration tool.
npcStick.behavior: Use on a block spawns an AC_EntityNPC above it; hitting an NPC with the item opens that NPC's configuration GUI.
npcStick.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Creates and edits AC_EntityNPC instances.

triggerStick.purpose: A trigger diagnostics and manual-pulse tool.
triggerStick.behavior: Left-clicking a block adds then removes a one-block trigger area to pulse it; right-clicking reports the trigger sources affecting the block.
triggerStick.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Directly calls the world's AdventureCraft trigger manager.

quill.behavior: Use on a block teleports the player to the first air block from the clicked Y through Y=128 at the block's centered X/Z; its pick range is 500 blocks.
quill.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation or its pick-range override.

paste.behavior: Use copies the completed Cursor region and writes it at the position calculated from the camera's look direction and reach; it records an undo region when history recording is active.
paste.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Requires a completed Cursor selection.
Its render integration previews the calculated paste position.

nudge.purpose: A destructive one-block region movement tool.
nudge.behavior: Right-click moves the completed Cursor region one block in the look-derived forward direction; left-click moves it in the opposite direction, and the selection follows the move.
nudge.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the item implementation.
interactions: Requires a completed Cursor selection.

AC_EntitySkeletonSword.purpose: A melee skeleton variant carrying a wooden sword.
AC_EntitySkeletonSword.behavior: It has damage 1, attacks within distance 2.5 on a 20-tick cooldown, and has no configured death-loot item.

AC_EntitySkeletonBoss.purpose: A large, high-health ranged skeleton boss variant.
AC_EntitySkeletonBoss.behavior: It is scaled to 2.5 times normal skeleton width/height, has 100 health, and fires five-arrow volleys at targets within 20 blocks on a 30-tick cooldown.
interactions: Its hurt override accepts damage only when the attacking entity is null; its fire-tick code applies such null-source damage.

AC_EntityBat.behavior: It has 5 health, seeks a nearby player within 100 blocks, alternates between wandering and moving to the target, and attacks at close range on a 10-tick cooldown.
AC_EntityBat.mode_notes: It removes itself when world difficulty is peaceful (difficulty 0).

AC_EntityRat.purpose: A small fast hostile ground creature.
AC_EntityRat.behavior: It has 6 health, damage 1, run speed 0.5, and targets the nearest visible player within 5 blocks.

AC_EntityNPC.purpose: A configurable non-player character built on the scripted-living-entity system.
AC_EntityNPC.behavior: By default it is non-pushable, non-attackable, tracks nearby visible players, and paths back toward its saved spawn position when more than two blocks away and not already pathing.
AC_EntityNPC.mode_notes: No debug-mode guard is present in the entity; the NPC Stick exposes creation and configuration.
interactions: Inherits configurable created, update, attacked, interaction, death, and path-reached scripts from AC_EntityLivingScript.
Can be assigned to NPC Path blocks.

AC_EntitySkeletonRifle.purpose: A skeleton variant armed with an Assault Rifle.
AC_EntitySkeletonRifle.behavior: It has damage 6, probabilistically fires one bullet with 0.07 spread when a target is within 15 blocks, uses 5-tick shot intervals and a 40-tick pause after each 30-shot magazine, and drops Assault Rifle Ammo as its loot ID.
interactions: Its held-item timer is updated for rifle firing/reload animation state.

AC_EntitySkeletonShotgun.purpose: A skeleton variant armed with a Shotgun.
AC_EntitySkeletonShotgun.behavior: It has damage 2, probabilistically fires eight bullets with 0.12 spread when a target is within 10 blocks, uses a 50-tick attack cooldown, and drops Shotgun Ammo as its loot ID.
interactions: Its held-item timer is updated for shotgun firing animation state.

AC_EntityZombiePistol.purpose: A zombie variant armed with a Pistol.
AC_EntityZombiePistol.behavior: It has damage 6, probabilistically fires one bullet with 0.08 spread whenever its inherited combat AI calls the attack check, uses a 20-tick shot interval and a 50-tick pause after each 15-shot magazine, and drops Pistol Ammo.
interactions: Its held-item timer is updated for pistol firing/reload animation state.

AC_EntityBomb.purpose: The live projectile/entity state of a thrown AdventureCraft Bomb.
AC_EntityBomb.behavior: It uses a 45-tick fuse, then deals distance-scaled damage up to 20 and knockback within 5 blocks and removes AC_BlockBombable blocks within radius 3; being hurt detonates it early.
interactions: Spawned by AC_ItemBomb and reused as the explosion implementation for Bomb Arrows.

AC_EntityBoomerang.purpose: The outbound and returning projectile state of the Boomerang item.
AC_EntityBoomerang.behavior: It flies outward and then homes back to its owner; it carries encountered item entities, stuns encountered mobs for 20 ticks, and toggles levers it crosses.
interactions: On removal it searches the owner's carried item, inventory, and nearby item entities to clear the matching Boomerang's active metadata.

AC_EntityArrowBomb.purpose: The projectile state of Bomb Arrow ammunition.
AC_EntityArrowBomb.behavior: It inherits arrow flight, emits smoke, and detonates through AC_EntityBomb after a 45-tick fuse or immediately when hurt; players cannot pick it up by touch.
interactions: Spawned by bows holding Bomb Arrows and by dispensers containing Bomb Arrows.

AC_EntityHookshot.purpose: The outbound, attached, and returning projectile state of the Hookshot item.
AC_EntityHookshot.behavior: It attaches to logs and AdventureCraft wood/wood half-step blocks, pulls its owner while attached, or grabs an encountered mob/item and carries it back while returning.
interactions: It is removed if its hand no longer contains the originating stack and is not persisted across a load.
An attached hook suppresses normal player gravity through MixinPlayerEntity.

AC_EntityLivingScript.purpose: A configurable living entity driven by an entity-description record and JavaScript callbacks.
AC_EntityLivingScript.behavior: Its description can set health, size, texture, movement speed, and callback script names; it runs created, per-tick update, attacked, interaction, death, and path-completed scripts and supports pathing to an entity or position.
interactions: The attacked and interaction scripts may return a Boolean to allow or deny the default action.
NPC extends this entity type.
